//: Example wiring for ~/.claude/settings.json. Merge the blocks you want. Hook order within a matcher does not affect safety — an exit-2 block from ANY hook always wins, and a hook 'allow' never overrides a settings deny rule. The bash-guards are OPINIONATED (worktree workflow + personal bash style); include only the ones you want.

permissions.//allow: Self-test allowlist so `bash tests/run.sh` / `tests/guards.test.sh` run prompt-free when developing this kit. `bash` is intentionally NOT wildcarded (that would defeat the permission system) — only these two scripts are allowed.
permissions.//allow-generic: OPTIONAL prompt-reducing baseline — keep only the rules you actually want. These are COARSE PREFIX matches: Bash(git:*) / Bash(gh:*) also cover WRITE subcommands (git commit, gh pr create), so enabling them is a conscious opt-in. The permissions.deny block below ALWAYS wins (git push --force, npm publish, curl|sh stay blocked) — that is the real backstop. The build/lang allows (python3, node, go, ...) pair with hooks/bash-guards/bare-interpreter-guard.sh, which stops an absolute interpreter path from slipping past a Bash(<name>:*) rule. auto-approve-readonly.sh already covers pure read-only commands; these add the WRITE-capable tools it deliberately defers.
